intent: Check whether the candidate can explain state ownership, controlled inputs, and predictable user feedback in a basic React form.
primaryQuestion: How would you build a React form where the input value, validation message, and submit button state stay predictable?

expectedSignals.label: Identifies which component owns the form state.
Explains controlled inputs and change handlers.
Mentions validation timing such as on change, blur, or submit.
Connects error display and disabled states to user feedback.

redFlags: Cannot explain where the input value is stored.
Relies on direct DOM reads as the main state model.
Ignores validation timing or user-visible error states.

scoringHints.description: Explains controlled state, validation timing, submit behavior, and user feedback with a concrete form example.
Covers controlled inputs and validation but misses one detail such as disabled submit or error clearing.
Gives a plausible form flow but keeps state ownership vague.
Answer is too generic to confirm practical React form understanding.

followUps.question: Can you walk through one field from typing to submit?
followUps.purpose: Force the answer into a concrete component state flow.

followUps.question: Where would the validation error live, and when would you clear it?
followUps.purpose: Clarify state ownership and user feedback behavior.

en.title: Predictable React form state
en.displayQuestion: How would you build a React form where the input value, validation message, and submit button state stay predictable?
en.displayIntent: Check controlled input and validation basics in React.
guidance: Use a concrete form field as the example.
Explain what state is stored and which component owns it.
commonMistakes: Only saying to use useState without explaining the flow.
Skipping validation and error display behavior.

vi.title: State form React dễ dự đoán
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn sẽ xây một form React như thế nào để giá trị input, thông báo validation và trạng thái nút submit luôn dễ dự đoán?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng controlled input và validation trong React.
guidance: Dùng một field cụ thể làm ví dụ.
Giải thích state nào được lưu và component nào sở hữu state đó.
commonMistakes: Chỉ nói dùng useState nhưng không giải thích luồng.
Bỏ qua validation và cách hiển thị lỗi.

ja.title: 予測しやすい React フォーム state
ja.displayQuestion: 入力値、validation message、submit button の状態を予測しやすく保つ React フォームをどのように作りますか。
ja.displayIntent: React の controlled input と validation の基礎を確認する。
guidance: 具体的なフォーム項目を例にする。
どの state をどの component が持つか説明する。
commonMistakes: useState と言うだけで流れを説明しない。
validation と error 表示を省略する。

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Sharing State Between Components
sourceReferences.note: Used for controlled/uncontrolled component and state ownership concepts.

sourceReferences.label: H5BP Front-end Developer Interview Questions
sourceReferences.note: Used for front-end interview coverage style; wording rewritten as an internal probe.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands React event handlers, passing callbacks, and preventing accidental invocation during render.
primaryQuestion: How do you wire a click handler in React, pass data to it, and avoid calling it during render by mistake?

expectedSignals.label: Defines event handlers as functions passed to JSX props.
Explains passing arguments through a wrapper callback.
Mentions event propagation or preventDefault when relevant.
Separates render-time code from interaction-time code.

redFlags: Calls the handler immediately in JSX without recognizing the issue.
Cannot explain how child components notify parents.
Ignores default browser behavior for form or link interactions.

scoringHints.description: Gives a concrete handler example, explains callback passing, and handles interaction semantics correctly.
Understands handlers but misses propagation or default behavior details.
Can describe click handling but confuses render and interaction timing.
Answer does not show practical JSX event handling knowledge.

followUps.question: What is the difference between onClick={handleClick} and onClick={handleClick()}?
How would a child button tell a parent which item was clicked?

en.title: React event handler wiring
en.displayQuestion: How do you wire a click handler in React, pass data to it, and avoid calling it during render by mistake?
en.displayIntent: Check React event handling and callback basics.
guidance: Use JSX examples if helpful.
Explain when the function runs.
commonMistakes: Invoking the handler during render.
Ignoring parent-child callback contracts.

vi.title: Gắn event handler trong React
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn gắn click handler trong React, truyền dữ liệu vào handler và tránh gọi nhầm handler ngay lúc render như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng event handling và callback trong React.
guidance: Có thể dùng ví dụ JSX.
Giải thích thời điểm function được chạy.
commonMistakes: Gọi handler ngay trong lúc render.
Bỏ qua contract callback giữa parent và child.

ja.title: React event handler の接続
ja.displayQuestion: React で click handler をつなぎ、data を渡し、render 中に誤って呼ばないようにするにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: React の event handling と callback の基礎を確認する。
guidance: 必要なら JSX 例を使う。
関数がいつ実行されるか説明する。
commonMistakes: render 中に handler を呼んでしまう。
parent-child callback contract を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Responding to Events
sourceReferences.note: Used for event handler and propagation concepts.

intent: Assess practical debugging of a non-working interaction in a React component.
primaryQuestion: A button looks enabled, but clicking it does nothing. How would you inspect the React component, event handler, and browser console to find the cause?

expectedSignals.label: Checks whether the handler is passed and bound correctly.
Looks for console errors and failed assumptions.
Verifies disabled state, overlay, event propagation, or conditional rendering.
Uses a small reproduction or React DevTools when useful.

redFlags: Starts rewriting the component before reproducing the failure.
Only guesses without checking console or props.
Ignores whether the UI is actually receiving the click.

scoringHints.description: Uses a systematic UI debugging flow from reproduction to handler verification and fix validation.
Covers handler and console checks but misses one UI cause such as overlay or disabled state.
Mentions generic debugging but lacks React-specific inspection.
No clear debugging sequence or evidence gathering.

followUps.question: How would you prove whether the click event reaches the button?
What would you check before changing the handler code?

en.title: Debug a broken React click
en.displayQuestion: A button looks enabled, but clicking it does nothing. How would you inspect the React component, event handler, and browser console to find the cause?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate practical debugging of React interactions.
guidance: Start from reproduction and observable evidence.
Mention component props, handler, and browser signals.
commonMistakes: Guessing a fix before confirming the event path.
Ignoring console errors or disabled state.

vi.title: Debug click React không chạy
vi.displayQuestion: Một button nhìn như đang enabled nhưng bấm không có gì xảy ra. Bạn kiểm tra component React, event handler và browser console như thế nào để tìm nguyên nhân?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng debug interaction React thực tế.
guidance: Bắt đầu từ reproduce và bằng chứng quan sát được.
Nhắc tới props, handler và tín hiệu từ browser.
commonMistakes: Đoán fix trước khi xác nhận đường đi của event.
Bỏ qua console error hoặc disabled state.

ja.title: React click が動かない問題の debug
ja.displayQuestion: button は enabled に見えるのに click しても何も起きません。React component、event handler、browser console をどう確認しますか。
ja.displayIntent: React interaction の実践的な debug 力を評価する。
guidance: 再現と観測できる証拠から始める。
props、handler、browser signal に触れる。
commonMistakes: event path を確認せずに修正を推測する。
console error や disabled state を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Responding to Events
sourceReferences.note: Used for event handler behavior and propagation.

sourceReferences.label: H5BP Front-end Developer Interview Questions
sourceReferences.note: Used for front-end debugging coverage style.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands list rendering, stable keys, and state preservation in React.
primaryQuestion: Why do React lists need stable keys, and what can go wrong if you use an array index for a list that can be filtered or reordered?

expectedSignals.label: Explains keys as identity for items across renders.
Connects unstable keys to incorrect preserved state.
Names safer key sources such as persistent IDs.
Mentions filtering, sorting, insertion, or deletion as risk cases.

redFlags: Says keys only silence warnings.
Always recommends array index keys without context.
Cannot connect keys to component identity or state.

scoringHints.description: Clearly explains identity, preserved state, and concrete reorder/filter failure modes.
Understands stable keys but gives limited examples.
Knows keys are required but cannot explain why.
Answer is too generic to assess list rendering knowledge.

followUps.question: Can you describe a bug caused by using index as key?
followUps.purpose: Test whether the candidate can connect keys to real UI behavior.

followUps.question: When might an index key be acceptable?

en.title: Stable keys in React lists
en.displayQuestion: Why do React lists need stable keys, and what can go wrong if you use an array index for a list that can be filtered or reordered?
en.displayIntent: Check understanding of list identity and state preservation.
guidance: Explain keys through an example list.
Connect the answer to filtering or reordering.
commonMistakes: Treating keys as only a warning fix.
Ignoring component state preservation.

vi.title: Key ổn định trong React list
vi.displayQuestion: Vì sao React list cần key ổn định, và điều gì có thể sai nếu dùng array index cho list có thể filter hoặc reorder?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về identity của list và preserved state.
guidance: Giải thích key qua một list cụ thể.
Gắn câu trả lời với filter hoặc reorder.
commonMistakes: Xem key chỉ là cách tắt warning.
Bỏ qua việc React preserve state theo identity.

ja.title: React list の stable key
ja.displayQuestion: React list になぜ stable key が必要で、filter や reorder される list で array index を key にすると何が起きますか。
ja.displayIntent: list identity と state preservation の理解を確認する。
guidance: 具体的な list 例で説明する。
filter や reorder と結びつける。
commonMistakes: key を warning 対応だけと考える。
state preservation を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Preserving and Resetting State
sourceReferences.note: Used for key and state preservation concepts.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can clarify missing UI requirements before implementing a React screen.
primaryQuestion: A React screen design only shows the desktop layout. What responsive behavior, keyboard access, and empty/loading/error states would you clarify before implementation?

expectedSignals.label: Asks about breakpoints and content wrapping.
Clarifies keyboard and focus behavior.
Identifies empty, loading, and error states.
Communicates assumptions before coding.

redFlags: Builds only the visible desktop mock without clarifying states.
Ignores keyboard or focus behavior.
Treats missing requirements as purely a design problem.

scoringHints.description: Clarifies responsive, accessibility, and state requirements with a practical implementation plan.
Covers most missing UI states but misses one accessibility or responsive detail.
Recognizes ambiguity but does not propose specific questions.
Answer does not show proactive requirement clarification.

followUps.question: What exact questions would you ask design or product before coding?
If product wants it shipped today, what minimum states would you still include?

en.title: Clarifying missing React UI states
en.displayQuestion: A React screen design only shows the desktop layout. What responsive behavior, keyboard access, and empty/loading/error states would you clarify before implementation?
en.displayIntent: Assess requirement clarification before building UI.
guidance: Name the missing states explicitly.
Explain who you would align with and why.
commonMistakes: Only building the visible desktop mock.
Forgetting keyboard and error states.

vi.title: Làm rõ UI state còn thiếu trong React
vi.displayQuestion: Design của một màn hình React chỉ có desktop layout. Bạn sẽ làm rõ responsive behavior, keyboard access và empty/loading/error state nào trước khi implement?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng làm rõ requirement trước khi build UI.
guidance: Nêu rõ các state còn thiếu.
Giải thích cần align với ai và vì sao.
commonMistakes: Chỉ build đúng desktop mock đang thấy.
Quên keyboard và error state.

ja.title: 不足している React UI state の確認
ja.displayQuestion: React screen design が desktop layout だけの場合、responsive behavior、keyboard access、empty/loading/error state の何を実装前に確認しますか。
ja.displayIntent: UI 実装前の requirement clarification を評価する。
guidance: 不足している state を明確に挙げる。
誰と何を合わせるか説明する。
commonMistakes: 見えている desktop mock だけを作る。
keyboard と error state を忘れる。

sourceReferences.label: H5BP Front-end Developer Interview Questions
sourceReferences.note: Used for accessibility, testing, and performance topic coverage.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can use design or code review feedback to improve React UI quality.
primaryQuestion: Tell me about a time feedback from design, product, or code review changed a React UI you built. What did you change and what did you learn?

expectedSignals.label: Describes a concrete feedback event.
Explains the React/UI change made afterward.
Shows collaboration without blame.
Mentions a lasting improvement in habit or quality.

redFlags: Cannot identify a concrete feedback example.
Frames feedback as purely subjective or annoying.
Does not explain what changed after the feedback.

scoringHints.description: Gives a concrete feedback story with action, outcome, and changed engineering habit.
Has a real example but outcome or lesson is light.
Acknowledges feedback but cannot show behavior change.
Answer is generic or not tied to personal work.

followUps.question: What was the specific screen and what feedback did you receive?
followUps.purpose: Ground the story in a real UI.

followUps.question: How did you know the revised UI was better?

en.title: React UI feedback story
en.displayQuestion: Tell me about a time feedback from design, product, or code review changed a React UI you built. What did you change and what did you learn?
en.displayIntent: Assess learning agility and collaboration around UI quality.
guidance: Use a real screen or component.
Include what changed afterward.
commonMistakes: Keeping the story generic.
Not naming the lesson learned.

vi.title: Câu chuyện nhận feedback UI React
vi.displayQuestion: Hãy kể về một lần feedback từ design, product hoặc code review làm thay đổi UI React bạn đã xây. Bạn đã đổi gì và học được gì?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá learning agility và collaboration quanh chất lượng UI.
guidance: Dùng một màn hình hoặc component thật.
Nêu điều đã thay đổi sau đó.
commonMistakes: Kể quá chung chung.
Không nêu bài học cụ thể.

ja.title: React UI feedback の経験
ja.displayQuestion: design、product、code review の feedback により、あなたが作った React UI が変わった経験を話してください。何を変え、何を学びましたか。
ja.displayIntent: UI 品質に関する learning agility と collaboration を評価する。
guidance: 実際の screen または component を使う。
その後に変わったことを含める。
commonMistakes: 話が抽象的すぎる。
学びを具体的に言わない。

sourceReferences.label: H5BP Front-end Developer Interview Questions
sourceReferences.note: Used for open-ended front-end interview style.

intent: Check practical understanding of useEffect as synchronization with external systems rather than general state derivation.
primaryQuestion: How do you decide whether logic belongs in render, an event handler, or a useEffect in a React component?

expectedSignals.label: Treats effects as synchronization with external systems.
Avoids effects for pure derived state when render can compute it.
Explains dependency arrays and cleanup.
Mentions user events as a separate place for interaction-specific logic.

redFlags: Uses useEffect as the default place for all logic.
Suppresses dependency linting without explaining why.
Cannot describe cleanup or resynchronization.

scoringHints.description: Distinguishes render, event, and effect logic with examples and dependency reasoning.
Understands effects but misses cleanup or dependency nuance.
Overuses effects or cannot explain resynchronization.
Answer lacks concrete React lifecycle reasoning.

followUps.question: Give an example of logic you would remove from useEffect and compute during render instead.
When is it risky to ignore the exhaustive-deps linter?

en.title: Render, event handler, or useEffect
en.displayQuestion: How do you decide whether logic belongs in render, an event handler, or a useEffect in a React component?
en.displayIntent: Check practical useEffect boundaries.
guidance: Compare at least two places for the same logic.
Mention dependencies and cleanup.
commonMistakes: Putting all logic in useEffect.
Ignoring dependency changes.

vi.title: Render, event handler hay useEffect
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn quyết định logic nên nằm trong render, event handler hay useEffect của một component React như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ranh giới sử dụng useEffect thực tế.
guidance: So sánh ít nhất hai nơi đặt cùng một logic.
Nhắc tới dependency và cleanup.
commonMistakes: Đưa mọi logic vào useEffect.
Bỏ qua dependency thay đổi.

ja.title: render、event handler、useEffect の使い分け
ja.displayQuestion: React component の logic を render、event handler、useEffect のどこに置くか、どう判断しますか。
ja.displayIntent: useEffect の実践的な境界を確認する。
guidance: 同じ logic の置き場所を比較する。
dependencies と cleanup に触れる。
commonMistakes: すべての logic を useEffect に入れる。
dependency change を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: React docs: useEffect
sourceReferences.note: Used for effect dependency and synchronization concepts.

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Lifecycle of Reactive Effects
sourceReferences.note: Used for effect lifecycle and resynchronization concepts.

intent: Evaluate debugging of repeated effects, stale closures, and accidental infinite render cycles.
primaryQuestion: A React component keeps fetching the same data repeatedly after a release. How would you determine whether the cause is effect dependencies, unstable objects/functions, or state updates inside the effect?

expectedSignals.label: Reproduces the repeated fetch and measures frequency.
Inspects dependency values and identity changes.
Checks whether the effect updates state that changes dependencies.
Verifies the fix without suppressing dependencies blindly.

redFlags: Immediately removes the dependency array or disables linting.
Cannot explain object or function identity in dependencies.
Does not check what state changes after each fetch.

scoringHints.description: Systematically narrows dependency identity, state update, and cleanup causes with verification.
Finds likely dependency causes but misses one verification step.
Recognizes useEffect but relies on trial-and-error fixes.
No concrete debugging path for repeated effects.

followUps.question: How would you prove the request count returned to normal?
Why might removing a dependency hide a bug?

en.title: Debug repeated React fetches
en.displayQuestion: A React component keeps fetching the same data repeatedly after a release. How would you determine whether the cause is effect dependencies, unstable objects/functions, or state updates inside the effect?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate debugging of effect dependency loops.
guidance: Mention reproduction and request counts.
Inspect dependency identity and state updates.
commonMistakes: Disabling dependency linting.
Changing code before observing the loop.

vi.title: Debug fetch React bị lặp
vi.displayQuestion: Một component React cứ fetch cùng dữ liệu lặp lại sau release. Bạn xác định nguyên nhân là dependency của effect, object/function không ổn định, hay state update bên trong effect như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng debug loop do effect dependency.
guidance: Nhắc tới reproduce và số request.
Kiểm tra identity của dependency và state update.
commonMistakes: Tắt dependency linting.
Sửa code trước khi quan sát loop.

ja.title: React fetch が繰り返される問題の debug
ja.displayQuestion: release 後、React component が同じ data を何度も fetch します。effect dependencies、unstable object/function、effect 内の state update のどれが原因かどう調べますか。
ja.displayIntent: effect dependency loop の debug 力を評価する。
guidance: 再現と request count に触れる。
dependency identity と state update を確認する。
commonMistakes: dependency linting を無効化する。
loop を観測する前に code を変える。

sourceReferences.label: React docs: useEffect
sourceReferences.note: Used for dependency and troubleshooting concepts.

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Lifecycle of Reactive Effects
sourceReferences.note: Used for resynchronization reasoning.

intent: Evaluate state management trade-off reasoning across local state, context, and Redux.
primaryQuestion: When would you keep state local in React, when would you lift it up, and when would you move it into Context or Redux?

expectedSignals.label: Considers state ownership and number of consumers.
Discusses update frequency and render impact.
Separates UI state, server cache, and business workflow state.
Mentions team conventions and testability.

redFlags: Always chooses Redux for every shared value.
Uses Context without considering update frequency.
Cannot distinguish local UI state from server data cache.

scoringHints.description: Compares local, lifted, Context, and Redux with realistic constraints and risks.
Shows good state ownership reasoning but misses performance or testability.
Has tool preference but weak trade-off explanation.
Answer is slogan-level and not actionable.

followUps.question: What would make Context a bad fit for this state?
Who needs to read or update this state?

en.title: Local state, Context, or Redux
en.displayQuestion: When would you keep state local in React, when would you lift it up, and when would you move it into Context or Redux?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate React state management trade-offs.
guidance: Compare alternatives under constraints.
Separate UI state from server data.
commonMistakes: Choosing one tool for every state.
Ignoring update frequency and ownership.

vi.title: Local state, Context hay Redux
vi.displayQuestion: Khi nào bạn giữ state local trong React, khi nào lift state lên, và khi nào đưa vào Context hoặc Redux?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá trade-off state management trong React.
guidance: So sánh lựa chọn theo constraint.
Tách UI state khỏi server data.
commonMistakes: Dùng một tool cho mọi loại state.
Bỏ qua update frequency và ownership.

ja.title: local state、Context、Redux の選択
ja.displayQuestion: React で state を local に保つ、lift up する、Context や Redux に移す判断はどう行いますか。
ja.displayIntent: React state management の trade-off を評価する。
guidance: 制約の中で選択肢を比較する。
UI state と server data を分ける。
commonMistakes: すべての state に同じ tool を使う。
update frequency と ownership を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Sharing State Between Components
sourceReferences.note: Used for lifting state and single source of truth concepts.

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Passing Data Deeply with Context
sourceReferences.note: Used for Context use cases and alternatives.

intent: Assess React performance reasoning around measurement, memoization, and stable references.
primaryQuestion: How would you decide whether to use useMemo or useCallback to reduce React render cost, and how would you verify it helped?

expectedSignals.label: Starts with profiling or observed render cost.
Explains cached calculations versus cached function identity.
Mentions dependency correctness and stale values.
Verifies outcome with profiler, timings, or user-visible metrics.

redFlags: Adds memoization everywhere without measurement.
Cannot distinguish useMemo from useCallback.
Ignores dependency correctness or stale closures.

scoringHints.description: Explains when memoization is useful, names risks, and verifies performance impact.
Understands memoization but lacks strong measurement detail.
Uses memoization as a default without cost-benefit reasoning.
No clear React performance model.

followUps.question: What metric or profiler signal would tell you the change helped?
What cost can unnecessary memoization add?

en.title: useMemo and useCallback trade-offs
en.displayQuestion: How would you decide whether to use useMemo or useCallback to reduce React render cost, and how would you verify it helped?
en.displayIntent: Assess React memoization and performance measurement.
guidance: Start with evidence of a render problem.
Explain dependencies and verification.
commonMistakes: Memoizing everything blindly.
Skipping performance measurement.

vi.title: Trade-off useMemo và useCallback
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn quyết định dùng useMemo hoặc useCallback để giảm chi phí render React như thế nào, và xác minh nó có ích ra sao?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá memoization và đo performance trong React.
guidance: Bắt đầu bằng bằng chứng có vấn đề render.
Giải thích dependency và cách verify.
commonMistakes: Memoize mọi thứ một cách mù quáng.
Bỏ qua đo performance.

ja.title: useMemo と useCallback の trade-off
ja.displayQuestion: React の render cost を下げるために useMemo または useCallback を使うか、どう判断し、効果をどう確認しますか。
ja.displayIntent: React memoization と performance measurement を評価する。
guidance: render 問題の証拠から始める。
dependencies と verification を説明する。
commonMistakes: 何でも memoize する。
performance measurement を省略する。

sourceReferences.label: React docs: useMemo
sourceReferences.note: Used for cached calculation and dependency concepts.

sourceReferences.label: React docs: useCallback
sourceReferences.note: Used for cached function identity concepts.

intent: Assess debugging of stale data caused by route changes, request races, or cache key mistakes.
primaryQuestion: A React route sometimes shows data from the previous record after navigation. What would you inspect in route params, fetching logic, cache keys, and cleanup behavior?

expectedSignals.label: Reproduces fast navigation or back/forward scenarios.
Checks route param changes and query/cache keys.
Considers request races and stale responses.
Validates loading and error states during transition.

redFlags: Only forces a full page reload as the fix.
Ignores request ordering or cancellation.
Does not inspect route params or cache identity.

scoringHints.description: Connects route identity, cache keys, race conditions, and UI transition states.
Finds likely stale data causes but lacks complete transition-state verification.
Guesses at state bugs without narrowing route or fetch behavior.
No systematic debugging plan.

followUps.question: What user action would you use to reproduce the stale data?
How would you verify that stale responses no longer win?

en.title: Debug stale data after navigation
en.displayQuestion: A React route sometimes shows data from the previous record after navigation. What would you inspect in route params, fetching logic, cache keys, and cleanup behavior?
en.displayIntent: Assess stale data debugging in route-driven React screens.
guidance: Mention route identity and request timing.
Include loading/error transition states.
commonMistakes: Fixing with full reload only.
Ignoring cache keys or stale responses.

vi.title: Debug stale data sau navigation
vi.displayQuestion: Một React route thỉnh thoảng hiển thị data của record trước sau khi navigate. Bạn kiểm tra route params, fetching logic, cache key và cleanup behavior như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá debug stale data trong màn hình React theo route.
guidance: Nhắc tới route identity và timing của request.
Bao gồm loading/error state khi transition.
commonMistakes: Chỉ fix bằng full reload.
Bỏ qua cache key hoặc stale response.

ja.title: navigation 後の stale data debug
ja.displayQuestion: React route が navigation 後に前の record data を表示することがあります。route params、fetching logic、cache keys、cleanup behavior の何を確認しますか。
ja.displayIntent: route-driven React screen の stale data debug を評価する。
guidance: route identity と request timing に触れる。

commonMistakes: full reload だけで直す。
cache key や stale response を無視する。

intent: Verify whether an accessibility improvement claim is concrete, tested, and tied to user impact.
primaryQuestion: Your CV says you improved accessibility in a React app. Which issue did you fix, how did you test it, and what changed for users?

expectedSignals.label: Names a specific accessibility issue or WCAG-related behavior.
Explains the React/component change made.
Mentions manual or automated testing.
Connects the fix to user impact or reduced defect risk.

redFlags: Only says accessibility was improved without naming an issue.
Cannot describe testing with keyboard, screen reader, or automated checks.
No personal contribution or outcome.

scoringHints.description: Provides specific issue, implementation, testing evidence, and user impact.
Concrete issue and fix are present but impact measurement is thin.
Claim sounds plausible but lacks testing or ownership evidence.
Cannot verify the CV claim from the answer.

followUps.question: What test or observable signal proved the accessibility issue was fixed?
What part of the fix did you personally implement?
followUps.purpose: Separate personal contribution from team work.

en.title: Verify React accessibility claim
en.displayQuestion: Your CV says you improved accessibility in a React app. Which issue did you fix, how did you test it, and what changed for users?
en.displayIntent: Verify concrete accessibility ownership and testing.
guidance: Name the issue and user impact.
Include test method and personal contribution.
commonMistakes: Using vague accessibility claims.
Skipping keyboard or assistive tech validation.

vi.title: Xác minh claim accessibility React
vi.displayQuestion: CV của bạn nói bạn cải thiện accessibility trong một app React. Bạn đã sửa issue nào, test ra sao và điều gì thay đổi với user?
vi.displayIntent: Xác minh ownership và testing accessibility cụ thể.
guidance: Nêu issue và tác động tới user.
Bao gồm cách test và đóng góp cá nhân.
commonMistakes: Nói claim accessibility quá mơ hồ.
Bỏ qua kiểm tra keyboard hoặc assistive tech.

ja.title: React accessibility claim の確認

ja.displayIntent: 具体的な accessibility ownership と testing を確認する。
guidance: issue と user impact を挙げる。
test method と personal contribution を含める。
commonMistakes: accessibility claim が曖昧すぎる。
keyboard や assistive tech の確認を省略する。

sourceReferences.label: H5BP Front-end Developer Interview Questions
sourceReferences.note: Used for accessibility and testing coverage topics.

intent: Assess component boundary design around props, composition, state ownership, and maintainability.
primaryQuestion: How would you split a complex React screen into reusable components without hiding important state or business rules in the wrong place?

expectedSignals.label: Identifies stable component responsibilities.
Defines clear prop and event contracts.
Keeps business rules near the right owner.
Uses composition rather than excessive prop drilling when appropriate.

redFlags: Splits by visual boxes only without data ownership.
Creates highly coupled components with implicit global assumptions.
Moves business rules into presentational components without reason.

scoringHints.description: Explains boundaries using state ownership, prop contracts, composition, and future change cost.
Good component split but misses one ownership or contract detail.
Mostly talks about file size or visual layout.
No concrete component design reasoning.

followUps.question: Which component would own the data and which would only render it?
When would you keep a component less reusable on purpose?

en.title: React component boundaries
en.displayQuestion: How would you split a complex React screen into reusable components without hiding important state or business rules in the wrong place?
en.displayIntent: Assess reusable component boundary design.
guidance: Use a concrete screen example.
Discuss props, events, and state ownership.
commonMistakes: Splitting only by visual layout.
Over-abstracting before reuse is real.

vi.title: Boundary component React
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn tách một màn hình React phức tạp thành các component reusable như thế nào mà không giấu state hoặc business rule sai chỗ?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá thiết kế boundary component có thể tái sử dụng.
guidance: Dùng một màn hình cụ thể.
Nói về props, event và state ownership.
commonMistakes: Chỉ tách theo layout nhìn thấy.
Trừu tượng hóa quá sớm khi chưa có reuse thật.

ja.title: React component boundary
ja.displayQuestion: 複雑な React screen を reusable component に分けるとき、重要な state や business rule を間違った場所に隠さないためにどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: reusable component boundary design を評価する。
guidance: 具体的な screen 例を使う。
props、event、state ownership を説明する。
commonMistakes: 見た目の layout だけで分ける。
実際の reuse 前に過度に抽象化する。

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Passing Data Deeply with Context
sourceReferences.note: Used for composition, prop drilling, and context alternatives.

sourceReferences.label: React docs: Sharing State Between Components

intent: Evaluate trade-offs between hand-written fetching in effects and a structured data fetching/cache library.
primaryQuestion: For a React dashboard with repeated API data, when would you keep fetching logic in custom hooks and when would you introduce a query/cache library?

expectedSignals.label: Considers duplicate requests, caching, invalidation, and retries.
Discusses loading and error state consistency.
Mentions bundle size, complexity, and team familiarity.
Defines signals that justify adopting a library.

redFlags: Chooses a library only because it is popular.
Ignores cache invalidation and stale data behavior.
Does not consider complexity added to a small app.

scoringHints.description: Compares custom hooks and cache libraries using concrete scale, correctness, and team constraints.
Good trade-off answer but misses one operational or user-state dimension.
Preference-driven answer without adoption criteria.
No clear trade-off or React data-fetching model.

followUps.question: What risk do you accept by adding a query/cache library?
What symptom would show your current custom hook approach is no longer enough?

en.title: Custom hooks or query cache
en.displayQuestion: For a React dashboard with repeated API data, when would you keep fetching logic in custom hooks and when would you introduce a query/cache library?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate React data fetching trade-offs.
guidance: Compare correctness and complexity.
Mention cache invalidation and user-visible states.
commonMistakes: Choosing tools by popularity only.
Ignoring stale data and retry behavior.

vi.title: Custom hook hay query cache
vi.displayQuestion: Với một React dashboard có nhiều API data lặp lại, khi nào bạn giữ fetching logic trong custom hook và khi nào dùng query/cache library?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá trade-off data fetching trong React.
guidance: So sánh correctness và complexity.
Nhắc tới cache invalidation và state user nhìn thấy.
commonMistakes: Chọn tool chỉ vì phổ biến.
Bỏ qua stale data và retry behavior.

ja.title: custom hook か query cache か

ja.displayIntent: React data fetching の trade-off を評価する。
guidance: correctness と complexity を比較する。
cache invalidation と user-visible states に触れる。
commonMistakes: 人気だけで tool を選ぶ。
stale data と retry behavior を無視する。

intent: Assess senior rendering model trade-off reasoning across client rendering, SSR, static rendering, and hydration cost.
primaryQuestion: For a React product page or dashboard, how would you choose between client-side rendering, server-side rendering, and static rendering?

expectedSignals.label: Considers SEO, first load, interactivity, and personalization.
Discusses caching and data freshness.
Mentions hydration and bundle cost.
Accounts for deployment complexity and team capability.

redFlags: Treats SSR as always faster or always better.
Ignores hydration and runtime JavaScript cost.
Does not separate product page needs from authenticated dashboard needs.

scoringHints.description: Compares rendering models against product constraints, performance, cache behavior, and operational cost.
Good model comparison but misses hydration or deployment complexity.
Uses simplified SEO/performance rules without nuance.
Cannot explain rendering model trade-offs.

followUps.question: What risk would you accept by choosing SSR for this screen?

en.title: Choosing a React rendering model
en.displayQuestion: For a React product page or dashboard, how would you choose between client-side rendering, server-side rendering, and static rendering?
en.displayIntent: Assess senior rendering model trade-offs.
guidance: Compare user, business, and operational constraints.
Mention hydration and cache behavior.
commonMistakes: Saying SSR is always faster.
Ignoring authenticated dashboard constraints.

vi.title: Chọn rendering model cho React

vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá trade-off rendering model ở mức senior.
guidance: So sánh constraint của user, business và vận hành.
Nhắc tới hydration và cache behavior.
commonMistakes: Nói SSR luôn nhanh hơn.
Bỏ qua constraint của authenticated dashboard.

guidance: user、business、operation の制約を比較する。
hydration と cache behavior に触れる。

authenticated dashboard の制約を無視する。

intent: Assess senior architecture for data fetching, cache consistency, loading states, and error recovery across a large React app.

expectedSignals.label: Defines ownership for server data versus UI state.

Standardizes loading, empty, and error states.

redFlags: Only describes one component-level fetch pattern.

Does not address consistency across teams or screens.

Good architecture but misses observability or migration detail.
Focuses on local hooks only without system-level consistency.
No senior-level data architecture reasoning.

followUps.question: What signals would tell you the data layer is reliable in production?

Include failure, cache, and rollout behavior.
commonMistakes: Only describing one component fetch.
Ignoring cache invalidation and observability.

Bao gồm failure, cache và rollout behavior.
commonMistakes: Chỉ mô tả fetch trong một component.

intent: Evaluate senior debugging across asynchronous React UI behavior, test reliability, and release confidence.
primaryQuestion: Tell me about a flaky Playwright or Cypress test for a React screen. How did you separate test flake from a real product or timing bug?

expectedSignals.label: Describes reproduction across local, CI, and timing conditions.
Inspects async UI behavior, network waits, and selectors.
Separates brittle test assumptions from product race conditions.
Explains fix verification and prevention.

redFlags: Deletes or skips the test without investigating.
Blames CI without evidence.
Cannot describe how the UI reaches a ready state.

scoringHints.description: Shows evidence-based diagnosis, clear product-versus-test separation, and durable prevention.
Good debugging story but prevention or release-risk decision is light.
Treats flakiness as test-only without product timing analysis.
No concrete flaky test example or resolution.

followUps.question: What exact UI readiness signal was the test waiting for?
How did you know the flake rate improved after the fix?

en.title: Debug flaky React E2E test
en.displayQuestion: Tell me about a flaky Playwright or Cypress test for a React screen. How did you separate test flake from a real product or timing bug?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate senior React test debugging and release confidence.
guidance: Use a real test failure story.
Explain evidence and prevention.
commonMistakes: Skipping the test without investigation.
Ignoring async UI readiness.

vi.title: Debug E2E test React bị flaky
vi.displayQuestion: Hãy kể về một Playwright hoặc Cypress test flaky cho màn hình React. Bạn tách test flake khỏi bug thật về product hoặc timing như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá debug test React và release confidence ở mức senior.
guidance: Dùng một câu chuyện test failure thật.
Giải thích bằng chứng và cách ngăn tái diễn.
commonMistakes: Skip test mà không điều tra.
Bỏ qua trạng thái UI sẵn sàng bất đồng bộ.

ja.title: flaky React E2E test の debug

ja.displayIntent: senior React test debugging と release confidence を評価する。
guidance: 実際の test failure の話を使う。
証拠と prevention を説明する。
commonMistakes: 調査せずに test を skip する。
async UI readiness を無視する。

intent: Verify senior ownership of a frontend architecture migration using decision ownership, rollout evidence, and measured impact.

expectedSignals.label: Separates personal decisions from team execution.
Describes migration strategy, compatibility, and rollback plan.
Mentions tests, CI, or incremental rollout.
Provides success metrics or operational outcomes.

redFlags: Uses 'we migrated' without naming personal decisions.
No rollback or compatibility plan.
No measurable outcome or risk reduction.

scoringHints.description: Verifiable senior migration ownership with strategy, rollout control, and measured results.
Real migration involvement but ownership or metrics are incomplete.
Likely participated but cannot show senior decision-making.
Claim cannot be validated from the answer.

followUps.question: Which migration decision would not have happened without you?
What baseline and final outcome proved the migration worked?

en.title: Verify React migration ownership

en.displayIntent: Verify senior frontend migration ownership.
guidance: Separate your decisions from team work.
Include rollout and measurement evidence.
commonMistakes: Using only 'we' language.
Skipping rollback and success metrics.

vi.title: Xác minh ownership migration React

vi.displayIntent: Xác minh ownership migration frontend ở mức senior.
guidance: Tách quyết định của bạn khỏi công việc của team.
Bao gồm bằng chứng rollout và đo lường.
commonMistakes: Chỉ dùng ngôn ngữ 'chúng tôi'.
Bỏ qua rollback và success metric.

ja.displayIntent: senior frontend migration ownership を確認する。
guidance: 自分の判断と team work を分ける。
rollout と measurement の証拠を含める。
commonMistakes: 「we」だけで話す。
rollback と success metrics を省略する。

intent: Assess senior collaboration when UX quality, engineering cost, accessibility, and delivery pressure conflict.
